approval_source: Current Codex session operator message approving companion defaults with amendments.

operator_decisions.companion_concept: Companions are runtime-linked identity profiles with visual, tone, status, and governed separate memory namespaces. They do not independently grant routing, tool access, memory write authority, or write authority.
operator_decisions.naming_policy: Names stay as Hermes/OpenClaw/Archon for now. Aliases can come later.
operator_decisions.visual_style: Use abstract runtime marks, status badges, borders, and lightweight animation presets until a brand pack exists.
operator_decisions.effect_scope: For v0.1, companion selection affects UI identity, tone preset, status narration, read-only runtime card display, and non-authoritative companion comments only.
operator_decisions.tone_level: Companion cards may include rarity, stats, and personality, but these are descriptive UX metadata only and must not change actual runtime capability until a future governed routing pass.
operator_decisions.memory_scope: Separate companion memory is allowed as governed, approval-gated, non-authoritative memory. This pass defines the namespace and validation boundary only; future memory writes require a separate approval/executor pass.
operator_decisions.switch_policy: Switching companions must use a read-only preview plus approval-gated write to the selected companion target.
operator_decisions.multi_companion_future: Future roster expansion should support one active companion first, then inactive roster browsing, then multiple visible companions later.
